title: mays
intro: Mays is another name for the common yarrow plant (Achillea millefolium), a flowering perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fern-like foliage and clusters of small white or yellow flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and ease digestive issues. The health benefits of yarrow include reducing menstrual cramps, improving circulation, and supporting immune function, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Yarrow can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
